campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the fees that businesses pay for their licenses in the city. Shannon Dragman from the Finance Department presented the proposed changes to these tax rates, and the council discussed and voted on whether to approve the new amounts.
keyTopics: Business license tax rate increases
Finance Department budget update
Municipal code amendments
City council deliberation and vote
Emergency preparedness office involvement

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing a new ordinance (city rule) and skipped the standard first reading process to speed things up. The ordinance is now scheduled to be approved at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for approval
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026, where they approved meeting minutes from earlier in the month and reviewed the city's financial situation for February. The council authorized the town manager to sign several construction and professional service contracts to move forward with various town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved financial and investment reports for February 2026
Authorized construction contracts with multiple vendors including QLM, Inc.
Approved town improvement projects and directed preparation of related plans
Authorized multi-year professional services agreements

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 29, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and finalize their upcoming agendas for May 5 and May 12, 2026. The council reviewed what items should be added to each agenda, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked that proper public notice requirements were met. They also reviewed public records from mid-to-late April.
keyTopics: Finalized May 5 meeting agenda and added any new items
Set time slots for specific agenda items to keep meetings on schedule
Reviewed public notification requirements for agenda items and documents
Prepared draft agenda for May 12 meeting
Reviewed public records from April 16-23, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and hear from residents during public comment. The main focus was reviewing and deciding on neighborhood grant requests for the 2026-27 budget year, along with discussing how often council members are attending neighborhood meetings.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27
Public comment period for residents
Council attendance at neighborhood meetings
Meeting minutes approval

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special study session on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for open positions on city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to observe the interviews and provide public comment both in person and online.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
In-person public comment opportunity
Online participation option for residents
Study session format (informal discussion)
Open seats on city boards and commissions

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 30, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 30, 2026 to approve meeting notes from two previous economic development discussions held in January and March. The council also opened the floor for residents to speak about any local issues for up to 3 minutes each, though they noted that state law may prevent them from making decisions on topics not officially listed on the agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of January 29, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Approval of March 27, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Public comment period for residents on jurisdiction matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be put on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The council also addressed a new three-year agreement with the firefighters union covering pay and working conditions.
keyTopics: Temporary large signs permitted around Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026 events
New firefighters union contract for 2026-2029
One agenda item was withdrawn by city staff before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held meetings to approve the city budget for 2026-27, update various fees and user charges, and reissue tax equity policies. The council also heard a presentation about Santa Clara County's plan to address homelessness in the community.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget and development fees approval
Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
User fees and charges updates
County homelessness prevention plan presentation
Approval of meeting minutes and upcoming calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to consider a proposal to divide a 1-acre vacant lot on Old Trace Road into nine separate residential lots, which would allow for the construction of nine new single-family homes and seven smaller accessory units. The council also handled routine business including meeting schedules and leadership elections.
keyTopics: Subdivision of Old Trace Road property into 9 residential lots
Approval of 9 new single-family homes and 7 junior accessory dwelling units
Project is exempt from environmental review under state law
Election of council chair and vice chair positions
